author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Status update 2026-06-23: QA red-team round filed four
bugs that block this epic's migration ticket
(`cc2fa96`):

- `e4e483b qa-title-validation` (bug) — titles with
  `\n` or `\0` accepted silently; corrupts `jjf ls`
  rows / loses data.
- `d1a01f0 qa-dep-validation` (bug) — `jjf dep add`
  accepts phantom targets and self-deps; the self-dep
  case is a one-line DoS against any open ticket.
- `a902492 qa-trailer-injection` (bug, blocked by
  `e4e483b`) — crafted titles land literal trailer
  lines in commit descriptions; parser strict today
  but injection surface real.
- `277f559 qa-concurrent-write-ux` (bug, soft-ship,
  not blocking migration) — race UX is unreadable
  jj-internal vomit.

Three of the four are blocks on `cc2fa96` because the
asterinas migration runs hand-written prose titles
through `jjf new` 968 times. Hitting any of these
during migration corrupts data silently.

The audit's "no schema gaps" verdict still holds; this
is a write-path-validation round, separate axis.

See `docs/handoffs/2026-06-23-qa-redteam.md` for the
session handoff.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
QA red-team round complete (2026-06-23). All four bugs shipped and closed:

- `e4e483b qa-title-validation` — commit `4aa787f`. Storage + CLI reject
  `\n` / `\0` / control chars in titles. `Error::InvalidTitle` +
  `invalid_title` envelope.
- `d1a01f0 qa-dep-validation` — commit `2348d16`. `Storage::add_dep_edge`
  rejects phantom targets (`issue_not_found`) and self-deps
  (`Error::SelfDependency` + `self_dependency` envelope).
- `a902492 qa-trailer-injection` — commit `1dce1ec`. Audit found
  `assignee` and `label` write paths weren't guarded against newlines;
  hardened. Generic `validate_no_newlines` helper. Added crafted-input
  defensive tests + a bookmark-wide trailer-block-shape walker.
- `277f559 qa-concurrent-write-ux` — commits `434a2f8` + `ad924ca`.
  `Error::ConcurrentWrite` typed at storage boundary; `Storage::mutate`
  and `add_comment` retry once with re-read on conflict; slug-claim
  fails fast and upgrades to `SlugCollision`. CLI envelopes documented.

Tests: 387 → 421 green (+34). `jjf ready --label epic:host-asterinas`
now surfaces `cc2fa96 host-asterinas-migrate` — the blocks edges
cleared and the migration is the next move.

Follow-up surfaced (subagent observation, worth filing): slug
uniqueness should be enforced at the bookmark level, not just
pre-flight. The QA test for slug-collision under racing writers
sometimes produces a divergent bookmark (two writes both succeed,
same slug, two heads) instead of one writer losing cleanly. The
merge-resolver's domain; not a regression introduced today but a
real edge.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
2026-06-23: Research ticket `487536a storage-out-of-tree-refs`
closed. Verdict: move every issue and memory off the `issues`
jj bookmark to per-issue git refs under `refs/jjf/issues/<id>`
and `refs/jjf/memories/<key>` (git-bug's pattern). Kills HEAD
drift at the source — the 4-CLI write dance is replaced by
`git update-ref` CAS.

Design doc at `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md`. Implementation
epic filed as `bd98097` with 8 sketched child tickets (write
path, read path, init, v2→v3 migrator, push/pull refspec,
snapshot cache, test sweep, self-host guard removal). Serial
dispatch, ~3–4 sessions.
